zh.Addington, Surrey, England: 英國素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵
zh.lifeSummary: 當Philips Chesney Lord Addington於1075年出生在英國素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵時，他的父親Ralph de Chesney31歲，母親Lady Maud de Waterville27歲。他於大約1100年與Mrs Phillip de Chesney在英國英格蘭素可史崔特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1109年在他的家鄉享年34歲。

zh-hans.Addington, Surrey, England: 英国素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philips Chesney Lord Addington于1075年出生在英国素瑞阿丁頓,第一代西德默斯子爵时，他的父亲Ralph de Chesney31岁，母亲Lady Maud de Waterville27岁。他于大约1100年与Mrs Phillip de Chesney在英国英格兰素可史崔特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1109年在他的家乡享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Chesney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Antrim, Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh, see McChesney .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Chaney  , a cognate of 3 below.
French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’). Compare Cheney 2.

variants.name: McChesney
meanings: Scottish (Galloway) and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh ‘son of the Saxon (i.e. the Englishman)’. The spelling seems to represent Mac Shasanaigh, to be pronounced originally as McHasney, rather than the English spelling ch.

variants.name: Chestnut
meanings: Scottish (Renfrewshire and Lanarkshire) and northern Irish: altered form of Chesney , shortened from McChesney . Its assimilation to the English word chestnut is due to folk etymology, the word itself being first recorded in 1519. The surname was probably taken to Ireland at the time of the 17th-century Plantation of Ulster.
English (Norfolk): probably a variant of Chestney (see Chaney ), altered by folk etymological association with the word chestnut.

variants.name: Chesna
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Chesney  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Addington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called in Old English as Eaddingtūn ‘settlement associated with Eadda’ or Æddingtūn ‘settlement associated with Æddi’. Places so named are found in Northamptonshire, Buckinghamshire, Kent, and Surrey.

nameMeanings.name: Philips
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Philip
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Philips Chesney Lord Addington was born in 1075, in Addington, Surrey, England, his father, Ralph de Chesney, was 31 and his mother, Lady Maud de Waterville, was 27. He married Mrs Phillip de Chesney about 1100, in Street, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1109, in his hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philips Chesney Lord Addington est né en 1075, à Addington, Surrey, Angleterre, son père, Ralph de Chesney, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Maud de Waterville, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Phillip de Chesney environ 1100, à Street, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1109, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philips Chesney Lord Addington 1075 in Addington, Surrey, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph de Chesney 31 und seine Mutter Lady Maud de Waterville 27. Er hat um 1100 in Street, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Mrs Phillip de Chesney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1109 im Alter von 34 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philips Chesney Lord Addington è nato il nel 1075 nel Addington, Surrey, England, il suo padre, Ralph de Chesney, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Lady Maud de Waterville, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Phillip de Chesney circa 1100, nel Street, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1109, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 34 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Philips Chesney Lord Addington(이)가 1075년 영국 수리 애딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de Chesney(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Maud de Waterville(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1100년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 스트릿에서 Mrs Phillip de Chesney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1109년 그의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philips Chesney Lord Addington nasceu em 1075, em Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph de Chesney, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Maud de Waterville, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Phillip de Chesney aproximadamente 1100, em Street, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1109, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philips Chesney Lord Addington родился в 1075 г. в местности аддингтон, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph de Chesney было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Maud de Waterville, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Phillip de Chesney около 1100 г. в местности стрит, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1109 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philips Chesney Lord Addington nació en 1075, en Addington, Surrey, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph de Chesney, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Maud de Waterville, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Mrs Phillip de Chesney aproximadamente 1100, en Street, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1109, en su pueblo, a la edad de 34 años.
